'''Oliver Pratt''' (born 4 September 2004) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} and winger for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Oliver Pratt - Rugby player profile & career statistics - Global Sports Archive |url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/people/rugby/oliver-pratt/7839995/ |access-date=28 March 2026 |website=globalsportsarchive.com}}</ref>

==Early Life==
Pratt was born in Yorkshire, England, and played for [[Heworth ARLFC]] as a junior.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2 January 2025 |title=Wakefield Trinity tie down rising York talent on new long-term deal |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/24828386.yorks-oli-pratt-signs-long-term-deal-wakefield-trinity/ |access-date=19 December 2025 |website=York Press |language=en}}</ref> During his youth career, he earned recognition as one of the region’s leading emerging talents and was selected for the Yorkshire origin squad in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web |title=York Knights announce signing of Wakefield Trinity outside back Oli Pratt |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61701/york-knights-announce-signing-of-wakefield-trinity-outside-back-oli-pratt |access-date=19 December 2025 |website=www.rugby-league.com |language=en}}</ref>

==Career==
Pratt progressed through the academy system at Wakefield Trinity and made his senior debut for the club in May 2023 in the Challenge Cup against Leigh Leopards. Later that year, he made his Super League debut as a teenager during Wakefield’s final match of the 2023 season. <ref>{{Cite web |title=Wakefield Trinity centre signs new long-term contract – Total Rugby League |url=https://www.totalrl.com/wakefield-trinity-centre-signs-new-long-term-contract/ |access-date=2026-05-10 |language=en-US}}</ref>

During the 2023 season, Pratt spent time on loan at the [[York Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]].                       In 2024, Pratt established himself as a key player for Wakefield during the club’s Championship campaign. He scored 18 tries in 34 appearances across all competitions and was named the Championship Young Player of the Year. His performances attracted significant praise for his consistency and attacking ability. 

Following Wakefield’s return to Super League, Pratt signed a new long-term contract with the club in December 2024, committing his future to Trinity for four additional years. Head coach Daryl Powell described him as a player capable of becoming a long-term Super League standout. <ref>{{Cite web |last=Hughes |first=Adam |date=31 December 2024 |title=Pratt Commits Future To Trinity |url=https://wakefieldtrinity.com/pratt-commits-future-to-trinity/ |access-date=19 December 2025 |website=Wakefield Trinity RLFC |language=en-GB}}</ref>
